name: Bran English Muffin
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water and set aside to proof.
2. Mix scalded milk and boiling water in a large bowl. Add honey and let stand until lukewarm.
3. Stir in yeast and whole wheat flour. Cover with a damp cloth and let stand in a warm, draft-free area until doubled.
4. Stir the sponge down.
5. Combine all-purpose flour, bran, salt, and baking soda, then blend into the sponge.
6. Turn out onto a floured surface and knead well (the dough should be workable but sticky). Return dough to bowl and let rise again for 25-30 minutes.
7. Grease 12 muffin rings (3 to 3 1/2-inch rounds).
8. Punch down the dough and roll or pat out to 1/2-inch thickness. Cut into circles and transfer with rings to a flat surface, cover, and let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
9. Dust both sides with cornmeal.
10. Toast on an ungreased griddle until browned, about 6-8 minutes per side, removing the ring after the first side is browned. Let muffins cool on a rack.
11. Separate into halves with a fork. Wrap well and refrigerate. Toast under broiler or in toaster oven just before serving.
